author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
title: How Christ is Our Mediator.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Of this flesh and blood Melchizedek also, when he blessed Abram himself, gave the testimony which is very well known to Christian believers, so that long afterwards it was said to Christ in the Psalms: “Thou art a Priest for ever, after the order of Melchizedek.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: This was not then an accomplished fact, but was still future; yet that faith of the fathers, which is the self-same faith as our own, used to chant it.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Now, to all who find death in Adam, Christ is of this avail, that He is the Mediator for life.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He is, however, not a Mediator, because He is equal with the Father; for in this respect He is Himself as far distant from us as the Father; and how can there be any medium where the distance is the very same?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Therefore the apostle does not say, “There is one Mediator between God and men, even Jesus Christ;” but his words are, “The Man Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: He is the Mediator, then, in that He is man,—inferior to the Father, by so much as He is nearer to ourselves, and superior to us, by so much as He is nearer to the Father.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: This is more openly expressed thus: “He is inferior to the Father, because in the form of a servant;” superior to us, because without spot of sin.
